# externs-phpparser

> Kotlin/JVM wrapper for PHP-Parser — parses PHP source files into AST text via managed external PHP binary.

`BinPhpParser` is the primary entry point. It extends `AbcBinary`, which handles process execution, timeout, and output caching. Call `execute()` to parse a PHP file. The result is a `BinaryResult` with an exit code and output file.

## Instructions for Agents

- Create `BinPhpParser()` and set `target` before calling `execute()`.
- Use `executeWith { }` for one-off config overrides — state restores automatically.
- Check `BinaryResult.code`: `0` = success, `-1` = timeout.
- Read AST output from `BinaryResult.output` (a `File`).
- Set `dumpType` to `DumpType.JSON` for machine-readable output, `S_EXPR` for structural, `VAR` for PHP debug.
- Set `doResolveName = true` for static analysis — resolves names to `Name_FullyQualified`.
- After resolution, `Name` nodes (`self`, `parent`, unqualified functions) need downstream handling.
- Set `doCacheOutput = true` to skip re-execution for identical commands.
- Catch `ExternalBinaryNotFoundException` at construction — thrown when no PHP binary is found.
- Catch `ExternalBinaryArgumentMissException` — thrown when `target` is not set before `execute()`.
- Never assume a specific PHP binary location. `BinPhpParser` resolves bundled or system PHP automatically.

## API Reference

- [Parser API](llms/parser.md): `BinPhpParser`, `AbcBinary`, `BinaryResult`, utility functions, exceptions.
- [PHP-Parser Guide](llms/php-parser-guide.md): Parsing pipeline, JSON format, name resolution, AST node types, error handling.
